title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
Setup


Code
if (!require(pacman)) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(
  plotly,
  crosstalk,
  DT,
  ggdist,
  ggridges,
  colorspace,
  gganimate,
  tidyverse,
  knitr
)

exam <- read_csv("Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
Data Preview


Code
glimpse(exam)


Rows: 322
Columns: 7
$ ID      <chr> "Student321", "Student305", "Student289", "Student227", "Stude…
$ CLASS   <chr> "3I", "3I", "3H", "3F", "3I", "3I", "3I", "3I", "3I", "3H", "3…
$ GENDER  <chr> "Male", "Female", "Male", "Male", "Male", "Female", "Male", "M…
$ RACE    <chr> "Malay", "Malay", "Chinese", "Chinese", "Malay", "Malay", "Chi…
$ ENGLISH <dbl> 21, 24, 26, 27, 27, 31, 31, 31, 33, 34, 34, 36, 36, 36, 37, 38…

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
2.1 Standard Error Bars


Code
ggplot(my_sum) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      x = RACE,
      ymin = mean - se,
      ymax = mean + se
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    colour = "black",
    alpha = 0.9,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = RACE,
      y = mean
    ),
    stat = "identity",
    color = "red",
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Standard Error of Mean Maths Score by Race",
    x = "Race",
    y = "Mean Maths score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
2.2 95% Confidence Interval


Code
ggplot(my_sum) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      x = reorder(RACE, -mean),
      ymin = mean - 1.96 * se,
      ymax = mean + 1.96 * se
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    colour = "black",
    alpha = 0.9,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = reorder(RACE, -mean),
      y = mean
    ),
    stat = "identity",
    color = "red",
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "95% Confidence Interval of Mean Maths Score by Race",
    x = "Race",
    y = "Mean Maths score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
3.1 99% Confidence Interval with Linked Table


Code
shared_df <- SharedData$new(my_sum)

p <- ggplot(shared_df) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      x = reorder(RACE, -mean),
      ymin = mean - 2.58 * se,
      ymax = mean + 2.58 * se
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    colour = "black",
    alpha = 0.9,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = RACE,
      y = mean,
      text = paste(
        "Race:", RACE,
        "<br>N:", n,
        "<br>Average score:", round(mean, 2),
        "<br>99% CI: [",
        round(mean - 2.58 * se, 2),
        ",",
        round(mean + 2.58 * se, 2),
        "]"
      )
    ),
    stat = "identity",
    color = "red",
    size = 1.5,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  xlab("Race") +
  ylab("Average Maths Score") +
  ggtitle("99% Confidence Interval of Average Maths Scores by Race") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      vjust = 0.5,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )


Warning in geom_point(aes(x = RACE, y = mean, text = paste("Race:", RACE, :
Ignoring unknown aesthetics: text


Code
bscols(
  widths = c(6, 6),
  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text"),
  DT::datatable(
    shared_df,
    rownames = FALSE,
    class = "compact",
    width = "100%",
    options = list(
      pageLength = 10,
      scrollX = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
    formatRound(
      columns = c("mean", "sd", "se"),
      digits = 2
    )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
4.1 Mean Point Interval Plot


Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = RACE,
      y = MATHS
    )
  ) +
  stat_pointinterval() +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising Confidence Intervals of Mean Maths Score",
    subtitle = "Mean point + multiple interval plot",
    x = "Race",
    y = "Maths score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
4.2 Median Point Interval Plot


Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = RACE,
      y = MATHS
    )
  ) +
  stat_pointinterval(
    .width = 0.95,
    .point = median,
    .interval = qi
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising Confidence Intervals of Median Maths Score",
    subtitle = "Median point + interval plot",
    x = "Race",
    y = "Maths score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
4.3 Multiple Interval Plot


Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = RACE,
      y = MATHS
    )
  ) +
  stat_pointinterval(
    .width = c(0.95, 0.99),
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising 95% and 99% Intervals of Mean Maths Score",
    subtitle = "Mean point + multiple interval plot",
    x = "Race",
    y = "Maths score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

title: Hands-on Exercise 4c
text:
Learning Reflection
In this exercise, I learned that uncertainty should not be hidden in visual analytics. Error bars, confidence intervals, and interval plots help communicate how reliable or uncertain a point estimate is. Interactive visualisation also helps users inspect the uncertainty values more clearly.

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
Overview
This hands-on exercise focuses on visualising distribution using ridgeline plots and raincloud plots.

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
1.1 Basic Ridgeline Plot


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = ENGLISH,
    y = CLASS
  )
) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    bandwidth = 3.4,
    fill = colorspace::lighten("#7097BB", 0.3),
    color = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    name = NULL,
    expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))
  ) +
  theme_ridges()

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
1.2 Ridgeline Plot with Gradient Fill


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = ENGLISH,
    y = CLASS,
    fill = after_stat(x)
  )
) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "English score",
    option = "C"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    name = NULL,
    expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))
  ) +
  theme_ridges()


Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
1.3 Ridgeline Plot with Tail Probability


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = ENGLISH,
    y = CLASS,
    fill = 0.5 - abs(0.5 - after_stat(ecdf))
  )
) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Tail probability",
    direction = -1
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    name = NULL,
    expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))
  ) +
  theme_ridges()


Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
1.4 Ridgeline Plot with Quartile Lines


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = ENGLISH,
    y = CLASS,
    fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))
  )
) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE,
    quantiles = 4,
    quantile_lines = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Quartiles") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    name = NULL,
    expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))
  ) +
  theme_ridges()


Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
1.5 Ridgeline Plot with 95% Probability Range


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = ENGLISH,
    y = CLASS,
    fill = factor(after_stat(quantile))
  )
) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE,
    quantiles = c(0.025, 0.975)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Probability",
    values = c("#FF0000A0", "#A0A0A0A0", "#0000FFA0"),
    labels = c("(0, 0.025]", "(0.025, 0.975]", "(0.975, 1]")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    name = NULL,
    expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))
  ) +
  theme_ridges()


Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
2.1 Half-eye Plot


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = RACE,
    y = ENGLISH
  )
) +
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.5,
    justification = -0.2,
    .width = 0,
    point_colour = NA
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Half-eye Plot of English Scores by Race",
    x = "Race",
    y = "English score"
  )

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
2.2 Half-eye Plot with Boxplot


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = RACE,
    y = ENGLISH
  )
) +
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.5,
    justification = -0.2,
    .width = 0,
    point_colour = NA
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.20,
    outlier.shape = NA
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "English Scores by Race",
    x = "Race",
    y = "English score"
  )

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
section: 2.3 Half-eye Plot with Boxplot and Dot Plot
text:
2.3 Half-eye Plot with Boxplot and Dot Plot


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = RACE,
    y = ENGLISH
  )
) +
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.5,
    justification = -0.2,
    .width = 0,
    point_colour = NA
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.20,
    outlier.shape = NA
  ) +
  stat_dots(
    side = "left",
    justification = 1.2,
    binwidth = 0.5,
    dotsize = 2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Raincloud-style Distribution of English Scores",
    x = "Race",
    y = "English score"
  )

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
2.4 Final Raincloud Plot


Code
ggplot(
  exam,
  aes(
    x = RACE,
    y = ENGLISH
  )
) +
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.5,
    justification = -0.2,
    .width = 0,
    point_colour = NA
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.20,
    outlier.shape = NA
  ) +
  stat_dots(
    side = "left",
    justification = 1.2,
    binwidth = 0.5,
    dotsize = 1.5
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_economist() +
  labs(
    title = "Raincloud Plot of English Scores by Race",
    x = "Race",
    y = "English score"
  )

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
Learning Reflection
In this exercise, I learned that ridgeline plots are useful for comparing score distributions across many groups, while raincloud plots combine density, boxplot, and raw observation views into one chart.
This hands-on exercise focuses on visualising distribution using ridgeline plots and raincloud plots.

    binwidth = 0.5,
    dotsize = 1.5
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_economist() +
  labs(
    title = "Raincloud Plot of English Scores by Race",
    x = "Race",
    y = "English score"
  )

title: Hands-on Exercise 4a
text:
Learning Reflection
In this exercise, I learned that ridgeline plots are useful for comparing score distributions across many groups, while raincloud plots combine density, boxplot, and raw observation views into one chart.

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text: This hands-on exercise recreates the key interactive visualisation techniques from Chapter 3 of R for Visual Analytics. The focus is on using ggiraph, plotly, DT, and crosstalk to make static R graphics interactive.

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text: This hands-on exercise recreates the key interactive visualisation techniques from Chapter 3 of R for Visual Analytics. The focus is on using ggiraph, plotly, DT, and crosstalk to make static R graphics interactive.

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
Setup


Code
if (!require(pacman)) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(
  ggiraph,
  plotly,
  patchwork,
  DT,
  tidyverse,
  crosstalk
)

exam_data <- read_csv("Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
1.1 Tooltip Showing Student ID


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = ID),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Maths Scores",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 7,
  height_svg = 4
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
1.2 Tooltip Showing Multiple Information


Code
exam_data <- exam_data %>%
  mutate(
    tooltip_text = paste0(
      "ID: ", ID,
      "\nClass: ", CLASS,
      "\nGender: ", GENDER,
      "\nRace: ", RACE,
      "\nMaths: ", MATHS
    )
  )

p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = tooltip_text),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Maths Score Distribution with Detailed Tooltip",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
1.4 Hover Effect by Class


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Hover Highlight by Class",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
1.5 Styled Hover Effect


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Styled Hover Highlight by Class",
    x = "Maths Score"

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4,

  )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
1.6 Tooltip and Hover Combined


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(
      tooltip = tooltip_text,
      data_id = CLASS
    ),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Tooltip and Hover Combined",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

girafe(
  ggobj = p,
  width_svg = 8,
  height_svg = 4,
  options = list(
    opts_tooltip(css = tooltip_css),
    opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
    opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")
  )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
1.7 Coordinated Multiple Views with ggiraph


Code
p1 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = ID, tooltip = tooltip_text),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Maths",
    x = "Maths Score"
  )

p2 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = ID, tooltip = tooltip_text),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "English",
    x = "English Score"
  )

girafe(
  code = print(p1 + p2),
  width_svg = 9,
  height_svg = 4,
  options = list(
    opts_tooltip(css = tooltip_css),
    opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
    opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")
  )
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:

Code
plot_ly(
  data = exam_data,

  y = ~ENGLISH,

)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:

Code
plot_ly(
  data = exam_data,
  x = ~ENGLISH,
  y = ~MATHS,
  color = ~RACE,
  type = "scatter",
  mode = "markers",
  text = ~paste(
    "ID:", ID,
    "<br>Class:", CLASS,
    "<br>Gender:", GENDER,

)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
2.3 ggplot Converted to Interactive Plotly


Code
p <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 100),
    ylim = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "English vs Maths",
    x = "Maths Score",
    y = "English Score"
  )

ggplotly(p)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3a
text:
2.4 Coordinated Multiple Views with plotly


Code
d <- highlight_key(exam_data)

p1 <- ggplot(d, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 100),
    ylim = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "English vs Maths",
    x = "Maths",
    y = "English"
  )

p2 <- ggplot(d, aes(x = MATHS, y = SCIENCE)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 100),
    ylim = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Science vs Maths",
    x = "Maths",
    y = "Science"
  )

subplot(
  ggplotly(p1),
  ggplotly(p2),
  titleX = TRUE,
  titleY = TRUE
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 3b
text:
This section extends Hands-on Exercise 3 by creating animated statistical graphics using gganimate and plotly.




Code
pacman::p_load(
  readxl,
  gifski,
  gapminder,
  plotly,
  gganimate,
  tidyverse
)







Code
globalPop <- read_xls("GlobalPopulation.xls", sheet = "Data") %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(Country, Continent), as.factor),
    Year = as.integer(Year)
  )







Code
ggplot(globalPop, aes(
  x = Old,
  y = Young,
  size = Population,
  colour = Country
)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = gapminder::country_colors) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Population Structure",
    x = "% Aged",
    y = "% Young"
  )














Code
p <- ggplot(globalPop, aes(
  x = Old,
  y = Young,
  size = Population,
  colour = Country
)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = gapminder::country_colors) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Year: {frame_time}",
    x = "% Aged",
    y = "% Young"
  ) +
  transition_time(Year) +
  ease_aes("linear")

animate(p, renderer = gifski_renderer())

text:

title: Hands-on Exercise 4d
text:
2.1 Computing Derived Fields


Code
df <- covid19 %>%
  mutate(
    rate = Death / Positive,
    rate.se = sqrt((rate * (1 - rate)) / Positive)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    rate > 0,
    Positive > 0,
    !is.na(rate),
    !is.na(rate.se)
  )

fit.mean <- weighted.mean(df$rate, 1 / df$rate.se^2)

title: Hands-on Exercise 4d
text:
2.2 Calculating Confidence Limits


Code
number.seq <- seq(1, max(df$Positive), 1)

number.ll95 <- fit.mean - 1.96 * sqrt((fit.mean * (1 - fit.mean)) / number.seq)
number.ul95 <- fit.mean + 1.96 * sqrt((fit.mean * (1 - fit.mean)) / number.seq)

number.ll999 <- fit.mean - 3.29 * sqrt((fit.mean * (1 - fit.mean)) / number.seq)
number.ul999 <- fit.mean + 3.29 * sqrt((fit.mean * (1 - fit.mean)) / number.seq)

dfCI <- data.frame(
  number.seq = number.seq,
  number.ll95 = pmax(0, number.ll95),
  number.ul95 = number.ul95,
  number.ll999 = pmax(0, number.ll999),
  number.ul999 = number.ul999,
  fit.mean = fit.mean
)

title: Hands-on Exercise 4d
text:
2.3 Static Funnel Plot


Code
p <- ggplot(
  df,
  aes(
    x = Positive,
    y = rate
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      label = `Sub-district`,
      text = paste(
        "Sub-district:", `Sub-district`,
        "<br>City:", City,
        "<br>District:", District,
        "<br>Positive cases:", Positive,
        "<br>Deaths:", Death,
        "<br>Fatality rate:", round(rate, 4)
      )
    ),
    alpha = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = dfCI,
    aes(
      x = number.seq,
      y = number.ll95
    ),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    colour = "grey40",
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = dfCI,
    aes(
      x = number.seq,
      y = number.ul95
    ),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    colour = "grey40",
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = dfCI,
    aes(
      x = number.seq,
      y = number.ll999
    ),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = dfCI,
    aes(
      x = number.seq,
      y = number.ul999
    ),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    data = dfCI,
    aes(yintercept = fit.mean),
    linewidth = 0.4,
    colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(0, 0.05)
  ) +
  ggtitle("Cumulative Fatality Rate by Cumulative Number of COVID-19 Cases") +
  xlab("Cumulative Number of COVID-19 Positive Cases") +
  ylab("Cumulative Fatality Rate") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )
